text_summary: A 58-year-old never-smoking Asian female with stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma was treated with first-line osimertinib. Baseline biomarkers included EGFR L858R, PD-L1 TPS 5%, no co-mutations in TP53 or STK11. The patient achieved a partial response (PR) by RECIST 1.1 at 8 weeks with 62% tumor shrinkage. Progression-free survival was 22 months. At progression, ctDNA analysis revealed acquired MET amplification (GCN 12) without EGFR C797S. Grade 2 rash and grade 1 diarrhea were the primary adverse events, managed with dose continuation. Brain MRI remained negative throughout treatment. The patient was subsequently enrolled on a clinical trial of osimertinib plus savolitinib for the MET-amplified resistance mechanism.
toxicities: grade 2 rash, grade 1 diarrhea
biomarkers_at_baseline: EGFR L858R, PD-L1 TPS 5%

text_summary: A 45-year-old male with BRAF V600E-mutant unresectable stage IIIC melanoma was treated with dabrafenib plus trametinib combination therapy. Baseline molecular profiling showed BRAF V600E, no NRAS co-mutation, CDKN2A loss, and TMB of 8 mut/Mb. The patient achieved complete response (CR) by 6 months. Treatment was continued for 24 months before disease progression in liver. At recurrence, biopsy showed NRAS Q61K acquisition. Pyrexia (grade 2) occurred in the first 3 months requiring dose interruption but resolved with rechallenge. The patient subsequently received nivolumab plus ipilimumab immunotherapy and achieved a durable partial response. Overall survival from initial diagnosis was 48 months and ongoing at last follow-up.
toxicities: grade 2 pyrexia, grade 1 fatigue
biomarkers_at_baseline: BRAF V600E, CDKN2A loss, TMB 8 mut/Mb

text_summary: A 52-year-old female with HER2-positive (IHC 3+, FISH amplified) metastatic breast cancer progressed on trastuzumab plus pertuzumab plus docetaxel first-line and T-DM1 second-line. She was treated with trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) as third-line therapy. Partial response was achieved at 6 weeks with 55% tumor shrinkage including regression of liver metastases. PFS was 18 months. Grade 2 nausea and grade 1 fatigue were the primary adverse events. Notably, the patient developed grade 1 interstitial lung disease (ILD) at month 9, managed with dose interruption and glucocorticoids with successful rechallenge. Baseline NGS showed PIK3CA E545K co-mutation. At progression after 18 months, HER2 expression remained 3+ but with acquired MET amplification.
toxicities: grade 2 nausea, grade 1 fatigue, grade 1 ILD
biomarkers_at_baseline: HER2 IHC 3+, FISH amplified, PIK3CA E545K

text_summary: A 62-year-old male with microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H) metastatic colorectal cancer was treated with first-line pembrolizumab monotherapy per KEYNOTE-177 results. Baseline molecular profile included MLH1 promoter hypermethylation (sporadic dMMR), BRAF V600E, TMB 42 mut/Mb, and PD-L1 CPS 65. The patient achieved complete response at 12 months with resolution of all liver and peritoneal metastases. Treatment was continued for 2 years total. At 36 months from initiation, the patient remained in sustained complete response off therapy. Grade 2 hypothyroidism (immune-related) developed at month 4 and required levothyroxine replacement. Grade 1 pruritis was managed with topical agents. This case demonstrates the exceptional durability of immunotherapy response in MSI-H CRC.
toxicities: grade 2 hypothyroidism (irAE), grade 1 pruritis
biomarkers_at_baseline: MSI-H, dMMR (MLH1 methylation), BRAF V600E, TMB 42 mut/Mb, PD-L1 CPS 65

text_summary: A 55-year-old female with BRCA1-mutant (germline c.5266dupC) stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian cancer achieved complete response after primary debulking surgery and 6 cycles of carboplatin/paclitaxel. She was started on olaparib maintenance therapy. The patient remained in complete response for 48 months on olaparib maintenance before developing platinum-sensitive recurrence in the pelvis. Grade 2 anemia was the primary toxicity, managed with dose reduction from 300mg BID to 250mg BID. Grade 1 nausea resolved after the first 3 months. At recurrence, tissue biopsy showed a BRCA1 reversion mutation restoring the open reading frame. The patient received subsequent platinum-based rechallenge but with limited response, consistent with restored homologous recombination from the reversion mutation.
toxicities: grade 2 anemia, grade 1 nausea
biomarkers_at_baseline: BRCA1 germline mutation (c.5266dupC), HRD score 58

text_summary: A 38-year-old never-smoking male with EML4-ALK fusion-positive stage IV lung adenocarcinoma with brain metastases was treated with first-line alectinib. Baseline profiling confirmed EML4-ALK variant 1 fusion by NGS, TP53 wild-type, PD-L1 TPS 0%. Brain MRI showed 3 small parenchymal metastases (largest 12mm). The patient achieved complete intracranial response by 8 weeks and systemic partial response with 70% tumor shrinkage. PFS was 32 months. Treatment was well tolerated with grade 1 constipation and grade 1 elevated CPK. At progression, tissue rebiopsy revealed ALK G1202R solvent-front resistance mutation. The patient transitioned to lorlatinib and achieved a second partial response. This case illustrates the paradigm of sequential ALK TKI therapy and the importance of resistance mutation profiling.
toxicities: grade 1 constipation, grade 1 elevated CPK
biomarkers_at_baseline: EML4-ALK fusion (variant 1), TP53 wild-type, PD-L1 TPS 0%

text_summary: A 63-year-old female with germline BRCA2-mutant metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma was treated with first-line FOLFIRINOX (8 cycles) achieving partial response. She was then transitioned to olaparib maintenance therapy per the POLO trial paradigm. The patient maintained stable disease on olaparib maintenance for 12 months with grade 1 fatigue and grade 1 nausea as the primary adverse events. At month 12, CT scan showed progressive disease in liver metastases. Overall survival from diagnosis of metastatic disease was 28 months. ctDNA analysis at progression showed the original BRCA2 mutation at reduced VAF but no reversion mutations detected. The patient was subsequently treated with gemcitabine plus nab-paclitaxel as second-line therapy. This case illustrates the role of olaparib maintenance after platinum response in BRCA-mutant pancreatic cancer.
toxicities: grade 1 fatigue, grade 1 nausea
biomarkers_at_baseline: BRCA2 germline mutation, KRAS G12D

text_summary: A 67-year-old male former smoker with KRAS G12C-mutant stage IV lung adenocarcinoma progressed on first-line carboplatin/pemetrexed/pembrolizumab after 8 months. He was treated with sotorasib as second-line therapy. The patient achieved partial response at 6 weeks with 40% tumor shrinkage. PFS was 8 months. Grade 2 diarrhea and grade 3 elevated ALT required dose interruption at month 3, with resumption at reduced dose after liver enzyme normalization. At progression, ctDNA revealed KRAS Y96D secondary mutation and MET amplification (GCN 8). STK11 and TP53 co-mutations were present at baseline and likely contributed to the limited immunotherapy benefit in the first-line setting. The patient was subsequently enrolled in a clinical trial evaluating a next-generation KRAS inhibitor.
toxicities: grade 2 diarrhea, grade 3 elevated ALT
biomarkers_at_baseline: KRAS G12C, STK11 mutation, TP53 mutation, PD-L1 TPS 1%

text_summary: A 74-year-old male with newly diagnosed IDH1 R132H-mutant AML, ineligible for intensive chemotherapy, was treated with ivosidenib plus azacitidine. Baseline characterization showed IDH1 R132H (VAF 38%), NPM1 wild-type, FLT3 wild-type, intermediate-risk cytogenetics. The patient achieved complete remission with incomplete hematologic recovery (CRi) by cycle 3. D-2-hydroxyglutarate levels decreased by 95%. The patient maintained CRi for 14 months before developing disease progression with blast increase. Differentiation syndrome (grade 2) occurred in cycle 1 requiring dexamethasone treatment. Grade 1 QTc prolongation was monitored but did not require dose modification. At relapse, repeat molecular testing revealed IDH2 R140Q emergence (isoform switch) as the resistance mechanism. This case demonstrates the efficacy and limitations of IDH-targeted therapy in elderly AML patients.
toxicities: grade 2 differentiation syndrome, grade 1 QTc prolongation
biomarkers_at_baseline: IDH1 R132H (VAF 38%), NPM1 wild-type, FLT3 wild-type

text_summary: A 56-year-old female with FGFR2-BICC1 fusion-positive intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, previously treated with gemcitabine plus cisplatin, was treated with pemigatinib. Molecular profiling confirmed FGFR2-BICC1 fusion by RNA-based NGS, with co-alterations in CDKN2A loss and BAP1 mutation. The patient achieved partial response at 8 weeks with 45% tumor shrinkage and significant improvement in cancer-related symptoms. PFS was 9 months. Grade 3 hyperphosphatemia was managed with phosphate binders and low-phosphate diet. Serous retinal detachment (grade 2) developed at month 5, requiring temporary dose hold with complete resolution. At progression, ctDNA analysis revealed an FGFR2 V564F gatekeeper mutation. The patient was transitioned to futibatinib (covalent FGFR inhibitor) with initial stable disease. This case illustrates the paradigm of targeted therapy in FGFR2 fusion-positive biliary tract cancer.
toxicities: grade 3 hyperphosphatemia, grade 2 serous retinal detachment
biomarkers_at_baseline: FGFR2-BICC1 fusion, CDKN2A loss, BAP1 mutation

text_summary: A 71-year-old male with BRCA2-mutant metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) was treated with olaparib after progression on enzalutamide and one prior taxane (docetaxel). Somatic tumor profiling confirmed biallelic BRCA2 loss (germline frameshift + somatic LOH), with ATM wild-type and low TMB (2 mut/Mb). The patient achieved confirmed partial response (PSA decline >50%, measurable disease regression) with radiographic improvement at 12 weeks. PFS was 10 months. Grade 2 anemia required transfusion support on two occasions. Grade 1 nausea was managed supportively. At progression, PSA rose with new bone metastases. The patient was enrolled on a trial of olaparib plus an AKT inhibitor. This case from the PROfound trial paradigm demonstrates olaparib activity in BRCA2-mutant mCRPC, the population with the strongest PARP inhibitor benefit in prostate cancer.
toxicities: grade 2 anemia (requiring transfusion), grade 1 nausea
biomarkers_at_baseline: BRCA2 biallelic loss (germline + somatic LOH), ATM wild-type, TMB 2 mut/Mb

text_summary: A 60-year-old male current smoker with stage IV lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR/ALK/ROS1 wild-type, PD-L1 TPS 30%, was treated with first-line carboplatin plus pemetrexed plus pembrolizumab followed by pembrolizumab plus pemetrexed maintenance. Molecular profiling showed KRAS G12V, TP53 R248W, STK11 wild-type, TMB 14 mut/Mb. The patient achieved partial response at 9 weeks. After 4 cycles of induction, transitioned to maintenance with ongoing PR. The patient developed grade 2 immune-mediated hepatitis at month 8, managed with pembrolizumab hold and prednisone taper, with successful rechallenge. PFS was 14 months. Progressive disease was detected in adrenal glands at month 14. Grade 1 fatigue and grade 1 nausea were present throughout treatment. Overall survival was 26 months. This case represents a typical outcome for PD-L1-intermediate NSCLC treated with chemoimmunotherapy.
therapy: carboplatin + pemetrexed + pembrolizumab, then pembrolizumab + pemetrexed maintenance
toxicities: grade 2 immune hepatitis (irAE), grade 1 fatigue, grade 1 nausea
biomarkers_at_baseline: KRAS G12V, TP53 R248W, STK11 wild-type, PD-L1 TPS 30%, TMB 14 mut/Mb

text_summary: A 49-year-old female with metastatic triple-negative breast cancer, previously treated with carboplatin/gemcitabine plus pembrolizumab and capecitabine, was treated with sacituzumab govitecan as third-line therapy. Baseline molecular profiling showed TROP2 high expression (H-score 280), BRCA1/2 wild-type, PD-L1 CPS 5, and TP53 R248W mutation. The patient achieved partial response at 8 weeks with 48% tumor shrinkage including regression of lung and lymph node metastases. PFS was 7 months. Grade 3 neutropenia occurred during cycle 2 requiring G-CSF support and dose delay. Grade 2 diarrhea was managed with loperamide. Grade 2 alopecia developed by cycle 3. UGT1A1 genotyping showed heterozygous *1/*28 status. At progression, new liver metastases appeared with maintained TROP2 expression. The patient was subsequently enrolled in a clinical trial of datopotamab deruxtecan.
toxicities: grade 3 neutropenia, grade 2 diarrhea, grade 2 alopecia
biomarkers_at_baseline: TROP2 high (H-score 280), BRCA wild-type, PD-L1 CPS 5, TP53 R248W

text_summary: A 68-year-old male with locally advanced cisplatin-eligible urothelial carcinoma of the bladder was treated with first-line enfortumab vedotin plus pembrolizumab per the EV-302 regimen. Baseline evaluation showed Nectin-4 expression (IHC 3+), PD-L1 CPS 25, FGFR3 wild-type, and TMB 12 mut/Mb. The patient achieved complete response by 6 months with resolution of all measurable disease including pelvic lymph node and lung metastases. CT imaging at 12 months confirmed sustained complete response. Grade 2 maculopapular rash developed during cycle 3, managed with topical corticosteroids. Grade 1 peripheral neuropathy was monitored without dose modification. Grade 2 hyperglycemia required metformin initiation. At 18 months, the patient remained in complete response on maintenance therapy. This case exemplifies the transformative efficacy of the ADC-immunotherapy combination in first-line urothelial carcinoma.
toxicities: grade 2 maculopapular rash, grade 1 peripheral neuropathy, grade 2 hyperglycemia
biomarkers_at_baseline: Nectin-4 IHC 3+, PD-L1 CPS 25, FGFR3 wild-type, TMB 12 mut/Mb

text_summary: A 61-year-old female with FRalpha-positive (PS2+ by VENTANA FOLR1 26B3), platinum-resistant high-grade serous ovarian cancer was treated with mirvetuximab soravtansine after progression on 3 prior lines including carboplatin/paclitaxel, bevacizumab, and pegylated liposomal doxorubicin. Baseline molecular profiling confirmed BRCA1/2 wild-type, TP53 mutation (S241F), and FRalpha PS2+ (strong staining in 85% of tumor cells). The patient achieved partial response at 9 weeks with 42% tumor shrinkage and a CA-125 decline from 890 to 128 U/mL. PFS was 6 months. Grade 2 blurred vision developed at month 2, requiring ophthalmologic evaluation and temporary dose hold (keratopathy on slit-lamp exam), with resolution after 2-week interruption. Grade 1 nausea was managed supportively. At progression, FRalpha expression was maintained, and the patient received subsequent chemotherapy.
toxicities: grade 2 blurred vision/keratopathy, grade 1 nausea, grade 1 fatigue
biomarkers_at_baseline: FRalpha PS2+ (85% strong staining), BRCA wild-type, TP53 S241F, CA-125 890 U/mL

text_summary: A 57-year-old male with CLDN18.2-positive (IHC: 80% moderate-to-strong staining), HER2-negative metastatic gastric adenocarcinoma was treated with first-line zolbetuximab plus mFOLFOX6. Baseline evaluation confirmed CLDN18.2 positivity by VENTANA CLDN18 (43-14A) assay, HER2 IHC 0, PD-L1 CPS 3, and MSS status. The patient achieved partial response at 12 weeks with 38% tumor shrinkage in liver metastases and primary gastric mass. PFS was 11 months. Nausea (grade 2) and vomiting (grade 2) were prominent during the first 3 infusion cycles, managed with antiemetics and pre-medication, with significant improvement by cycle 4. Grade 1 decreased appetite persisted throughout treatment. At progression, new peritoneal disease developed. CA 19-9 showed initial decline from 450 to 85 U/mL followed by gradual rise at month 9. The patient received second-line ramucirumab plus paclitaxel.
toxicities: grade 2 nausea, grade 2 vomiting, grade 1 decreased appetite
biomarkers_at_baseline: CLDN18.2 positive (80% moderate-strong), HER2 IHC 0, PD-L1 CPS 3, MSS

text_summary: A 64-year-old male with previously treated extensive-stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC) was treated with tarlatamab (10mg IV Q2W) after progression on carboplatin/etoposide plus atezolizumab and subsequent topotecan. Baseline evaluation showed DLL3 expression by IHC (H-score 210), brain MRI negative, LDH elevated at 580 U/L. The patient developed grade 2 cytokine release syndrome (CRS) during the first dose (fever 39.1C, hypotension), managed with IV fluids, dexamethasone, and acetaminophen, with resolution within 24 hours. Step-up dosing was completed without further CRS. The patient achieved partial response at 8 weeks with 45% tumor shrinkage. PFS was 5 months. Grade 1 ICANS (confusion) occurred during cycle 2, resolving spontaneously. At progression, new brain metastases were identified. Overall survival from tarlatamab initiation was 12 months, representing meaningful benefit in a heavily pretreated SCLC population.
toxicities: grade 2 CRS, grade 1 ICANS, grade 1 fatigue
biomarkers_at_baseline: DLL3 IHC H-score 210, LDH 580 U/L, brain MRI negative

text_summary: A 59-year-old female with relapsed EZH2-mutant (Y646N) follicular lymphoma (grade 1-2), previously treated with R-CHOP and bendamustine-rituximab, was treated with tazemetostat 800mg BID orally. Baseline EZH2 testing confirmed Y646N mutation by cobas EZH2 Mutation Test. Baseline LDH was normal, and PET/CT showed FDG-avid disease in cervical, axillary, and inguinal lymph nodes. The patient achieved complete response by 6 months as assessed by PET/CT (Deauville score 2) with resolution of all FDG-avid disease. Response was maintained for 14 months on continuous tazemetostat therapy. Grade 1 nausea and grade 1 fatigue were the primary adverse events, both well managed without dose modification. No secondary malignancies or cytopenias developed. This case demonstrates the favorable efficacy and tolerability profile of EZH2 inhibition in EZH2-mutant follicular lymphoma, where the ORR of 69% exceeds that of the EZH2 wild-type population.
toxicities: grade 1 nausea, grade 1 fatigue
biomarkers_at_baseline: EZH2 Y646N mutation, LDH normal, follicular lymphoma grade 1-2

text_summary: A 54-year-old postmenopausal female with PIK3CA H1047R-mutant, HR-positive/HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer was treated with capivasertib plus fulvestrant after progression on letrozole plus ribociclib. Baseline molecular profiling confirmed PIK3CA H1047R by FoundationOne CDx, ESR1 wild-type by ctDNA, and PTEN intact by IHC. The patient achieved partial response at 12 weeks with 35% tumor shrinkage in liver and bone metastases. PFS was 8 months. Grade 2 diarrhea developed during the first cycle, managed with loperamide and improved with the intermittent dosing schedule (4 days on/3 days off). Grade 2 maculopapular rash affected the trunk and was managed with topical corticosteroids. Grade 1 hyperglycemia required glucose monitoring without pharmacologic intervention. At progression, ctDNA revealed emergence of an ESR1 D538G mutation. The patient was transitioned to elacestrant as subsequent therapy.
toxicities: grade 2 diarrhea, grade 2 maculopapular rash, grade 1 hyperglycemia
biomarkers_at_baseline: PIK3CA H1047R, ESR1 wild-type, PTEN intact, HR-positive/HER2-negative

text_summary: A 66-year-old female with HER2-low (IHC 1+) metastatic breast cancer, HR-positive, previously treated with letrozole plus palbociclib followed by fulvestrant plus abemaciclib, was treated with trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) per the DESTINY-Breast04 paradigm. Baseline molecular profiling showed HER2 IHC 1+, FISH non-amplified, PIK3CA wild-type, ESR1 D538G by ctDNA. The patient achieved partial response at 9 weeks with 52% tumor shrinkage in liver, lung, and bone metastases. PFS was 12 months. Grade 1 nausea was managed with ondansetron. Grade 1 fatigue was intermittent. Surveillance chest CT at month 6 showed new ground-glass opacities; high-resolution CT and pulmonary function tests led to a diagnosis of grade 1 ILD, managed with treatment interruption and close monitoring, with resolution over 4 weeks and successful T-DXd rechallenge at full dose. This case illustrates the meaningful efficacy of T-DXd in the HER2-low population, which was previously not considered HER2-actionable.
toxicities: grade 1 nausea, grade 1 fatigue, grade 1 ILD (resolved with interruption)
biomarkers_at_baseline: HER2 IHC 1+ (HER2-low), FISH non-amplified, ESR1 D538G, PIK3CA wild-type, HR-positive

text_summary: A 56-year-old female with relapsed FLT3-ITD-positive AML was treated with gilteritinib after failing induction with daunorubicin plus cytarabine and midostaurin followed by allogeneic stem cell transplant. Baseline characterization showed FLT3-ITD with allelic ratio 0.72, NPM1 mutation (co-occurring), and normal karyotype. The patient achieved composite complete remission (CRc) by cycle 2 with FLT3-ITD clearance from peripheral blood by cycle 3. Duration of CRc was 8 months before relapse with rising blasts. Grade 3 elevated AST/ALT required dose reduction from 120mg to 80mg daily. Differentiation syndrome (grade 1) was managed with dexamethasone. At relapse, FLT3 F691L gatekeeper resistance mutation was detected. The patient was bridged to a second allogeneic transplant using conditioning with fludarabine/busulfan. This case illustrates gilteritinib as a bridge-to-transplant strategy in relapsed FLT3-mutant AML.
toxicities: grade 3 elevated AST/ALT, grade 1 differentiation syndrome
biomarkers_at_baseline: FLT3-ITD (allelic ratio 0.72), NPM1 mutation, normal karyotype

text_summary: A 78-year-old male with newly diagnosed AML with IDH2 R140Q mutation, ineligible for intensive chemotherapy due to ECOG PS 2 and cardiac comorbidities, was treated with venetoclax plus azacitidine. Baseline bone marrow showed 45% blasts with IDH2 R140Q (VAF 42%), SRSF2 P95H co-mutation, and intermediate-risk cytogenetics. The patient achieved complete remission with incomplete count recovery (CRi) by cycle 2 with IDH2 mutation VAF declining to 5%. Transfusion independence was achieved by cycle 4. The patient maintained CRi for 18 months. Grade 4 neutropenia required cycle delays and dose modifications. Tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis was administered per ramp-up schedule. Febrile neutropenia occurred in cycle 1 requiring hospitalization. At month 18, progressive disease with new IDH2 R172K mutation was detected. OS was 24 months. This case demonstrates the efficacy of venetoclax-azacitidine in IDH2-mutant AML, a subgroup with particularly favorable responses.
toxicities: grade 4 neutropenia, febrile neutropenia, tumor lysis syndrome risk
biomarkers_at_baseline: IDH2 R140Q (VAF 42%), SRSF2 P95H, intermediate cytogenetics

text_summary: A 42-year-old female with newly diagnosed chronic-phase chronic myeloid leukemia (CML) was treated with imatinib 400mg daily. Baseline evaluation showed WBC 145,000/uL, Philadelphia chromosome positive by cytogenetics, BCR-ABL1 p210 transcript by RT-PCR. The patient achieved complete hematologic response by 3 months and complete cytogenetic response (CCyR) by 12 months. Major molecular response (MMR, BCR-ABL1 IS <=0.1%) was achieved by 18 months. By 5 years, the patient achieved deep molecular response (MR4.5, BCR-ABL1 IS <=0.0032%). After maintaining MR4.5 for 3 consecutive years, treatment-free remission (TFR) was attempted per the EURO-SKI criteria. At 24 months off therapy, the patient remained in molecular remission with monthly BCR-ABL1 monitoring. Key adverse events during treatment included grade 1 periorbital edema, grade 1 muscle cramps, and grade 1 nausea, all manageable. This case exemplifies the transformative impact of imatinib in CML, converting a fatal disease to one where treatment discontinuation is feasible.
toxicities: grade 1 periorbital edema, grade 1 muscle cramps, grade 1 nausea
biomarkers_at_baseline: BCR-ABL1 p210, Philadelphia chromosome positive

text_summary: A 55-year-old male with chronic-phase CML resistant to imatinib and dasatinib was treated with ponatinib. The patient had developed a BCR-ABL1 T315I gatekeeper mutation, conferring resistance to all first- and second-generation TKIs. Baseline BCR-ABL1 IS was 35% with T315I detected by Sanger sequencing. Ponatinib 45mg daily was initiated. The patient achieved CCyR by 6 months and MMR by 12 months. BCR-ABL1 T315I became undetectable by 9 months. Due to cardiovascular risk, ponatinib was dose-reduced to 15mg daily after achieving MMR, per the OPTIC trial dose-optimization strategy. The patient developed grade 2 hypertension requiring amlodipine and grade 1 dry skin. No arterial occlusive events occurred with dose reduction and cardiovascular risk management. At 36 months, the patient maintained MMR on ponatinib 15mg. This case demonstrates ponatinib as the only approved TKI active against T315I CML and the importance of dose optimization for cardiovascular safety.
toxicities: grade 2 hypertension, grade 1 dry skin
biomarkers_at_baseline: BCR-ABL1 T315I mutation, BCR-ABL1 IS 35%

text_summary: A 69-year-old male with nonmetastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (nmCRPC) and PSA doubling time of 4.2 months was treated with enzalutamide plus ADT per PROSPER trial data. Baseline evaluation showed PSA 18.5 ng/mL, conventional imaging (bone scan and CT) negative for metastases, testosterone <50 ng/dL on LHRH agonist. PSA declined to nadir of 0.4 ng/mL by month 6. The patient remained metastasis-free for 38 months before developing a single bone metastasis in L4 vertebra detected on bone scan. Enzalutamide was well tolerated with grade 1 fatigue and grade 1 hot flashes. No seizures or cognitive changes were observed. At development of metastatic disease, the patient was transitioned to docetaxel plus continued ADT. Overall survival from enzalutamide initiation was 62 months. This case demonstrates the significant delay in metastatic progression with enzalutamide in high-risk nmCRPC.
toxicities: grade 1 fatigue, grade 1 hot flashes
biomarkers_at_baseline: PSA 18.5 ng/mL, PSA DT 4.2 months, conventional imaging negative

text_summary: A 72-year-old male with PSMA-positive mCRPC was treated with 177Lu-PSMA-617 after progression on enzalutamide, abiraterone, and docetaxel. 68Ga-PSMA-11 PET/CT confirmed high PSMA uptake (SUVmax 38) in multiple bone and lymph node metastases with no PSMA-negative discordant lesions on FDG PET. The patient received 6 cycles of 177Lu-PSMA-617 (7.4 GBq per cycle, every 6 weeks). PSA declined by 72% from baseline (from 185 to 52 ng/mL). Bone scan showed mixed response with some lesions showing sclerotic healing. Best overall response was partial response by RECIST 1.1 in measurable lymph node disease. PFS was 9 months. Grade 2 dry mouth persisted from cycle 2. Grade 2 fatigue was intermittent. Grade 3 thrombocytopenia occurred after cycle 5, requiring cycle 6 delay. No grade 3+ anemia. OS from 177Lu-PSMA-617 initiation was 16 months. This case demonstrates the efficacy of radioligand therapy in heavily pretreated PSMA-positive mCRPC.
toxicities: grade 2 dry mouth, grade 2 fatigue, grade 3 thrombocytopenia
biomarkers_at_baseline: PSMA-positive (SUVmax 38), PSA 185 ng/mL

text_summary: A 63-year-old male with unresectable hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC), Child-Pugh A5, hepatitis B etiology (on entecavir), was treated with first-line atezolizumab plus bevacizumab per IMbrave150. Baseline evaluation showed BCLC stage C with portal vein invasion, AFP 1,250 ng/mL, no esophageal varices on endoscopy. The patient achieved partial response at 12 weeks with 45% tumor shrinkage and AFP decline from 1,250 to 85 ng/mL. PFS was 10 months. Grade 2 hypertension was managed with amlodipine. Grade 1 proteinuria was monitored without dose modification. No GI bleeding events occurred. Grade 2 immune-related hypothyroidism developed at month 4 requiring levothyroxine. At progression, new intrahepatic lesions appeared with AFP rising. The patient received second-line lenvatinib. OS from treatment initiation was 22 months. This case demonstrates the efficacy of I/O-anti-VEGF combination in HBV-associated HCC with portal vein invasion.
toxicities: grade 2 hypertension, grade 1 proteinuria, grade 2 hypothyroidism (irAE)
biomarkers_at_baseline: AFP 1250 ng/mL, Child-Pugh A5, HBV etiology, portal vein invasion

text_summary: A 58-year-old male with IMDC intermediate-risk metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma was treated with first-line nivolumab plus ipilimumab per CheckMate-214. Baseline evaluation showed lung, bone, and adrenal metastases, sarcomatoid features (20%), PD-L1 positive (>=1%), prior nephrectomy. The patient received 4 doses of ipilimumab 1mg/kg plus nivolumab 3mg/kg followed by nivolumab maintenance. Complete response was achieved by month 9 with resolution of all measurable disease. Nivolumab was continued for 2 years total then discontinued. Grade 3 immune-mediated colitis occurred at cycle 3 requiring IV methylprednisolone and infliximab, with resolution. Grade 2 hypothyroidism required levothyroxine. At 36 months off therapy, the patient remained in sustained complete response. This case demonstrates the potential for durable treatment-free remission with dual checkpoint blockade in mRCC, particularly in patients with sarcomatoid features and PD-L1 expression.
toxicities: grade 3 immune colitis, grade 2 hypothyroidism
biomarkers_at_baseline: Clear cell histology, sarcomatoid features 20%, PD-L1 positive, IMDC intermediate risk

text_summary: A 65-year-old female with IMDC favorable-risk metastatic clear cell RCC was treated with first-line pembrolizumab plus axitinib per KEYNOTE-426. Baseline evaluation showed lung-only metastases, VHL mutation, PBRM1 loss, no sarcomatoid features, PD-L1 CPS 5. The patient achieved partial response at 9 weeks with 58% tumor shrinkage. PFS was 20 months on combination therapy. Axitinib dose was titrated up to 7mg BID based on tolerability. Grade 2 diarrhea was managed with loperamide. Grade 2 hand-foot syndrome required dose reduction of axitinib to 3mg BID at month 6. Grade 1 immune-related thyroiditis progressed to hypothyroidism requiring replacement. At progression, new liver metastases appeared. The patient received second-line cabozantinib. OS from diagnosis was 42 months and ongoing. This case demonstrates the efficacy of I/O-TKI combination across all IMDC risk groups including favorable risk.
toxicities: grade 2 diarrhea, grade 2 hand-foot syndrome, grade 1 thyroiditis
biomarkers_at_baseline: Clear cell, VHL mutation, PBRM1 loss, PD-L1 CPS 5, IMDC favorable

text_summary: A 51-year-old male with metastatic gastrointestinal stromal tumor (GIST) harboring KIT exon 11 deletion (p.W557_K558del) with hepatic metastases was treated with first-line imatinib 400mg daily. Baseline molecular profiling confirmed KIT exon 11 in-frame deletion by NGS. CT at 8 weeks showed partial response by RECIST and Choi criteria (40% tumor density reduction in liver metastases). PFS was 30 months. Imatinib was well tolerated with grade 1 periorbital edema, grade 1 nausea, and grade 1 muscle cramps. At progression, CT showed growth of liver lesions with new peritoneal nodules. Repeat biopsy revealed secondary KIT exon 17 D816V mutation conferring imatinib resistance. The patient was switched to sunitinib second-line with stable disease for 8 months. This case illustrates the typical response pattern of KIT exon 11-mutant GIST to imatinib and the emergence of secondary resistance mutations.
toxicities: grade 1 periorbital edema, grade 1 nausea, grade 1 muscle cramps
biomarkers_at_baseline: KIT exon 11 deletion (W557_K558del)

text_summary: A 62-year-old female with unresectable GIST harboring PDGFRA D842V mutation was treated with avapritinib per NAVIGATOR trial data. This mutation confers primary resistance to imatinib. Baseline CT showed a 12cm gastric GIST with peritoneal implants. Avapritinib 300mg daily was initiated. The patient achieved partial response at 8 weeks with 55% tumor shrinkage by RECIST and dramatic Choi response (72% density reduction). By 12 months, near-complete response was observed with residual 2cm gastric mass. ctDNA showed PDGFRA D842V clearance by month 3. PFS exceeded 24 months and was ongoing at last follow-up. Grade 2 periorbital edema was managed with diuretics. Grade 2 cognitive effects (word-finding difficulty, memory impairment) developed at month 4, improving with dose reduction to 200mg. Grade 1 nausea was intermittent. This case demonstrates the unprecedented efficacy of avapritinib in PDGFRA D842V GIST, a previously untreatable molecular subtype.
toxicities: grade 2 periorbital edema, grade 2 cognitive effects, grade 1 nausea
biomarkers_at_baseline: PDGFRA D842V mutation

text_summary: A 48-year-old female with persistent metastatic cervical squamous cell carcinoma (HPV-16 positive) was treated with first-line pembrolizumab plus carboplatin plus paclitaxel plus bevacizumab per KEYNOTE-826. Baseline evaluation showed PD-L1 CPS 35, lung and para-aortic lymph node metastases, PIK3CA E545K mutation by NGS. The patient achieved partial response at 9 weeks with 52% tumor shrinkage. Chemotherapy was completed for 6 cycles, then pembrolizumab plus bevacizumab maintenance was continued. PFS was 14 months. Grade 3 neutropenia during cycle 3 required G-CSF. Grade 2 hypertension from bevacizumab was managed with lisinopril. Grade 1 immune-related rash resolved spontaneously. At progression, new hepatic metastases appeared. The patient received second-line tisotumab vedotin. OS was 26 months. This case demonstrates the efficacy of pembrolizumab-chemotherapy in PD-L1-positive advanced cervical cancer.
toxicities: grade 3 neutropenia, grade 2 hypertension, grade 1 immune rash
biomarkers_at_baseline: PD-L1 CPS 35, HPV-16 positive, PIK3CA E545K

text_summary: A 61-year-old male former smoker with recurrent metastatic oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma (HPV-positive, p16+) was treated with first-line pembrolizumab monotherapy based on PD-L1 CPS 45 per KEYNOTE-048. Baseline evaluation showed bilateral cervical lymphadenopathy and lung metastases, TP53 wild-type, PIK3CA H1047R, TMB 8 mut/Mb. The patient achieved partial response at 12 weeks with 48% tumor shrinkage. Response deepened over 6 months to near-complete response. PFS was 16 months. Grade 2 immune-related hepatitis at month 5 required pembrolizumab hold and prednisone taper; successful rechallenge after resolution. Grade 1 fatigue was ongoing. At progression (new pulmonary nodules), the patient received platinum-based chemotherapy. OS was 30 months. HPV-positive HNSCC is associated with higher response rates to immunotherapy, consistent with this patient's durable response to pembrolizumab monotherapy.
cancer_type: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma
toxicities: grade 2 immune hepatitis, grade 1 fatigue
biomarkers_at_baseline: PD-L1 CPS 45, HPV-positive (p16+), PIK3CA H1047R, TMB 8 mut/Mb

text_summary: A 67-year-old female with primary advanced (stage IIIC2) dMMR/MSI-H endometrial endometrioid adenocarcinoma was treated with dostarlimab plus carboplatin plus paclitaxel per RUBY trial, followed by dostarlimab maintenance. Baseline evaluation showed MLH1/PMS2 loss by IHC (MLH1 promoter hypermethylation), high TMB (48 mut/Mb), PTEN loss, and ARID1A mutation. The patient achieved complete response by cycle 6 of chemoimmunotherapy with resolution of pelvic, para-aortic lymphadenopathy and peritoneal disease. Dostarlimab maintenance was continued. PFS exceeded 24 months and was ongoing. Grade 2 immune-related diarrhea/colitis at month 3 was managed with budesonide. Grade 2 hypothyroidism required levothyroxine. Grade 1 arthralgia was intermittent. This case demonstrates the profound efficacy of checkpoint inhibitor-chemotherapy in dMMR endometrial cancer, where the 24-month PFS rate of 61% dramatically exceeds chemotherapy alone.
therapy: dostarlimab + carboplatin + paclitaxel, then dostarlimab maintenance
toxicities: grade 2 immune colitis, grade 2 hypothyroidism, grade 1 arthralgia
biomarkers_at_baseline: dMMR (MLH1/PMS2 loss), MSI-H, TMB 48 mut/Mb, PTEN loss, ARID1A mutation

text_summary: A 65-year-old male with occupational asbestos exposure presenting with unresectable malignant pleural mesothelioma (epithelioid histology) was treated with first-line nivolumab plus ipilimumab per CheckMate-743. Baseline evaluation showed right-sided pleural thickening with mediastinal lymphadenopathy, BAP1 loss by IHC, CDKN2A homozygous deletion, TMB 3 mut/Mb. The patient achieved partial response at 12 weeks with significant pleural thickening reduction and improved dyspnea. PFS was 8 months. Grade 2 immune-related pneumonitis at month 4 required treatment hold and oral prednisone taper over 6 weeks, with successful rechallenge. Grade 1 pruritis and grade 1 fatigue were ongoing. At progression, new contralateral pleural disease appeared. The patient received second-line pemetrexed plus cisplatin. OS was 20 months. This case demonstrates immunotherapy benefit in epithelioid mesothelioma, a disease previously limited to chemotherapy.
toxicities: grade 2 immune pneumonitis, grade 1 pruritis, grade 1 fatigue
biomarkers_at_baseline: BAP1 loss, CDKN2A deletion, TMB 3 mut/Mb, epithelioid histology

text_summary: A 68-year-old male with JAK2 V617F-positive primary myelofibrosis (DIPSS intermediate-2 risk) presenting with massive splenomegaly (spleen 25cm), constitutional symptoms (night sweats, weight loss, fatigue), and anemia (Hgb 9.2 g/dL) was treated with ruxolitinib per COMFORT data. Baseline evaluation showed JAK2 V617F (VAF 62%), grade 2 bone marrow fibrosis, platelet count 220K, circulating blasts 1%. Ruxolitinib 20mg BID was initiated. The patient achieved 42% spleen volume reduction by week 24 (measured by MRI) with marked improvement in constitutional symptoms and MFSAF total symptom score reduction of 55%. Hemoglobin stabilized at 10.1 g/dL after initial drop to 8.5 g/dL requiring transfusion support for 8 weeks. Platelet count remained stable. At 36 months, the patient maintained spleen response with improved quality of life. JAK2 V617F VAF decreased to 38%. This case illustrates the symptomatic and spleen response benefit of ruxolitinib in intermediate/high-risk MF.
toxicities: grade 2 anemia (transient transfusion dependence), grade 1 thrombocytopenia
biomarkers_at_baseline: JAK2 V617F (VAF 62%), grade 2 fibrosis, DIPSS intermediate-2

text_summary: A 72-year-old male with relapsed CLL harboring del(17p) and TP53 mutation (R248W), previously treated with fludarabine-cyclophosphamide-rituximab (FCR), was treated with ibrutinib. Baseline evaluation showed bulky lymphadenopathy, lymphocyte count 85K, beta-2-microglobulin 5.8 mg/L, unmutated IGHV. The patient developed treatment-related lymphocytosis peaking at 180K during week 4 (expected pharmacodynamic effect of BTK inhibition mobilizing CLL cells from lymph nodes). Lymph node response was rapid with 60% reduction by week 8. Partial response by iwCLL criteria was achieved by month 6. PFS was 42 months. Grade 2 atrial fibrillation developed at month 18 requiring rate control with metoprolol; ibrutinib was continued. Grade 1 bruising and grade 1 diarrhea were ongoing. At progression, BTK C481S mutation was detected. The patient was switched to venetoclax plus rituximab. OS was 56 months. This case demonstrates ibrutinib efficacy in high-risk del(17p) CLL and the expected treatment-related lymphocytosis.
toxicities: grade 2 atrial fibrillation, grade 1 bruising, grade 1 diarrhea
biomarkers_at_baseline: del(17p), TP53 R248W, unmutated IGHV, beta-2-microglobulin 5.8 mg/L

text_summary: A 70-year-old male with unresectable HCC (Child-Pugh A6, non-viral etiology/NASH-related), with high-risk esophageal varices precluding bevacizumab use, was treated with durvalumab plus tremelimumab (STRIDE regimen) per HIMALAYA. Baseline AFP was 380 ng/mL, BCLC stage B (multinodular), PD-L1 unknown. The single tremelimumab 300mg priming dose was administered with durvalumab 1500mg, followed by durvalumab Q4W. Partial response was achieved at 12 weeks with 35% reduction in total target lesion size and AFP decline to 65 ng/mL. PFS was 7 months. Grade 2 immune-related hepatitis required a 2-week hold at month 3 with resolution. Grade 1 pruritis and grade 1 fatigue were ongoing. No variceal bleeding occurred. At progression, the patient received lenvatinib. OS was 18 months. This case demonstrates the STRIDE regimen as a viable first-line option for HCC patients ineligible for bevacizumab.
toxicities: grade 2 immune hepatitis, grade 1 pruritis, grade 1 fatigue
biomarkers_at_baseline: AFP 380 ng/mL, Child-Pugh A6, NASH etiology, esophageal varices

text_summary: A 59-year-old female with stage IVB pMMR/MSS endometrial serous carcinoma was treated with pembrolizumab plus carboplatin plus paclitaxel per NRG-GY018, followed by pembrolizumab maintenance. Baseline evaluation showed peritoneal carcinomatosis and liver metastases, TP53 R175H (hotspot), ERBB2 amplification (HER2 IHC 2+/FISH amplified), pMMR by IHC, TMB 4 mut/Mb. The patient achieved partial response by cycle 3 with 48% tumor shrinkage. PFS was 15 months on pembrolizumab-chemotherapy followed by maintenance. Grade 3 neutropenia during induction required G-CSF. Grade 2 peripheral neuropathy from paclitaxel persisted after chemotherapy completion. Grade 1 immune-related hypothyroidism. At progression, the patient was enrolled on a trastuzumab deruxtecan trial given HER2 amplification. OS was 28 months. This case demonstrates that pembrolizumab-chemotherapy benefits even pMMR endometrial cancer, extending beyond the dMMR population.
therapy: pembrolizumab + carboplatin + paclitaxel, then pembrolizumab maintenance
toxicities: grade 3 neutropenia, grade 2 peripheral neuropathy, grade 1 hypothyroidism
biomarkers_at_baseline: pMMR/MSS, TP53 R175H, ERBB2 amplified, TMB 4 mut/Mb

text_summary: A 58-year-old male with germline BRCA2-mutant metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma completed 16 cycles of first-line FOLFIRINOX achieving confirmed partial response with 50% tumor shrinkage in hepatic metastases. Transitioned to olaparib maintenance per POLO trial paradigm. The patient maintained stable disease on olaparib for 8 months with improved quality of life compared to active chemotherapy. CA 19-9 remained stable at 45 U/mL during maintenance. Grade 1 nausea was the primary adverse event; no dose reductions were required. At month 8, rising CA 19-9 to 320 U/mL preceded radiographic progression by 6 weeks. CT showed new peritoneal nodules. OS from initial diagnosis was 26 months. The patient was enrolled in a clinical trial combining PARP inhibitor with an ATR inhibitor. This case illustrates the maintenance role of olaparib in BRCA-mutant pancreatic cancer, providing chemotherapy-free interval with preserved quality of life.
toxicities: grade 1 nausea
biomarkers_at_baseline: Germline BRCA2 mutation, KRAS G12V, CDKN2A loss
